title: European Court Confirms Ethical Veganism Is a Protected Philosophical Belief in Europe
excerpt: On 16th July 2026, the European Court of Human Rights (ECHR) ruled that Switzerland violated the fundamental rights of two vegans who were not consistently provided with vegan meals while held in…
content_html:
On 16th July 2026, the European Court of Human Rights (ECHR) ruled that Switzerland violated the fundamental rights of two vegans who were not consistently provided with vegan meals while held in detention facilities, confirming that ethical veganism and anti-speciesism are protected philosophical beliefs within Europe. 

This court is part of the Council of Europe and not the EU, and therefore Switzerland, a non-EU European country, is also covered by it (as are the UK, Norway and Turkey).  The Swiss case started with an animal rights activist being arrested in November 2018 for his direct-action activities and placed in pre-trial detention at Geneva’s Champ-Dollon prison for 11 months. He took the government to court because he was not offered a sufficiently nutritious vegan diet. An appeal was deemed inadmissible by Switzerland’s federal court in June 2020, after which the prisoner’s lawyer took the case to the Strasbourg-based European Court of Human Rights. That is when a former patient at a Swiss psychiatric hospital unit joined the appeal, which was dealt with by the ECHR as it had already accepted the case. 

In October 2022, the court asked the Swiss state to consider whether the Geneva prison had violated Article 9 of the European Convention of Human Rights, which states that “everyone has the right to freedom of thought, conscience and religion.” The Court has now confirmed that it did. 

The judgment found that the authorities had breached the right to freedom of thought, conscience, and religion, as well as the right to an effective remedy. The paragraph 126 of the judgement states, “In the circumstances of the present case, and having regard to the above comparative research, the Court considers that, faced with the applicants’ consistent and genuine vegan beliefs and their requests for a vegan diet while deprived of liberty, the authorities were under a positive obligation stemming from Article 9 to address those requests in substance and within a clear domestic legal framework. Failing this, it is difficult to consider that the authorities have done what was necessary to strike a fair balance between the competing interests at stake, having regard to the aims mentioned in the second paragraph of Article 9 (see paragraph 118 above). As to the factors to be taken into consideration when examining such requests, they include the applicants’ interest but also any possible organisational, financial or practical constraints for the authorities.”

This case now adds to the other legal cases where veganism has been accepted as a protected characteristic, as has been the case in the UK since 2020, when a judge in Norwich Employment Tribunal first ruled that ethical veganism is a philosophical belief under the Equality Act 2010. The ECHR judges mentioned this case in their written judgement, as well as a 2021 case in Bologna, Italy, that was also influenced by the UK case.

sentences.zh: 2026 年 7 月 16 日，欧洲人权法院 (ECHR) 裁定，瑞士侵犯了两名纯素食者的基本权利，这两名素食者在拘留设施中没有始终如一地获得纯素餐点，确认道德纯素食主义和反物种主义是欧洲境内受保护的哲学信仰。

That is when a former patient at a Swiss psychiatric hospital unit joined the appeal, which was dealt with by the ECHR as it had already accepted the case.

sentences.en: That is when a former patient at a Swiss psychiatric hospital unit joined the appeal, which was dealt with by the ECHR as it had already accepted the case.

sentences.en: In October 2022, the court asked the Swiss state to consider whether the Geneva prison had violated Article 9 of the European Convention of Human Rights, which states that “everyone has the right to freedom of thought, conscience and religion.”
sentences.zh: 2022年10月，法院要求瑞士政府考虑日内瓦监狱是否违反了《欧洲人权公约》第9条，该条规定“每个人都有思想、良心和宗教自由的权利”。

sentences.en: The Court has now confirmed that it did.
sentences.zh: 法院现已确认确实如此。

The paragraph 126 of the judgement states, “In the circumstances of the present case, and having regard to the above comparative research, the Court considers that, faced with the applicants’ consistent and genuine vegan beliefs and their requests for a vegan diet while deprived of liberty, the authorities were under a positive obligation stemming from Article 9 to address those requests in substance and within a clear domestic legal framework. As to the factors to be taken into consideration when examining such requests, they include the applicants’ interest but also any possible organisational, financial or practical constraints for the authorities.”

sentences.en: The judgment found that the authorities had breached the right to freedom of thought, conscience, and religion, as well as the right to an effective remedy.
sentences.zh: 判决认为，当局侵犯了思想、良心和宗教自由的权利，以及获得有效补救的权利。

sentences.en: The paragraph 126 of the judgement states, “In the circumstances of the present case, and having regard to the above comparative research, the Court considers that, faced with the applicants’ consistent and genuine vegan beliefs and their requests for a vegan diet while deprived of liberty, the authorities were under a positive obligation stemming from Article 9 to address those requests in substance and within a clear domestic legal framework.
sentences.zh: 判决书第126段指出：“在本案的情况下，并考虑到上述比较研究，法院认为，面对申请人一贯和真正的纯素信仰及其在被剥夺自由期间对纯素饮食的要求，当局有第9条规定的积极义务，在明确的国内法律框架内实质性地解决这些要求。

sentences.en: Failing this, it is difficult to consider that the authorities have done what was necessary to strike a fair balance between the competing interests at stake, having regard to the aims mentioned in the second paragraph of Article 9 (see paragraph 118 above).
sentences.zh: 如果做不到这一点，考虑到第 9 条第 2 款提到的目标（见上文第 118 段），很难认为当局已采取必要措施，在利害攸关的利益之间取得公平平衡。

sentences.en: As to the factors to be taken into consideration when examining such requests, they include the applicants’ interest but also any possible organisational, financial or practical constraints for the authorities.”
sentences.zh: 至于审查此类请求时要考虑的因素，包括申请人的利益，但也包括当局可能面临的组织、财务或实际限制。”

blocks.html_en: This case now adds to the other legal cases where veganism has been accepted as a protected characteristic, as has been the case in the UK since 2020, when a judge in Norwich Employment Tribunal first ruled that ethical veganism is a philosophical belief under the Equality Act 2010. The ECHR judges mentioned this case in their written judgement, as well as a 2021 case in Bologna, Italy, that was also influenced by the UK case.

sentences.en: This case now adds to the other legal cases where veganism has been accepted as a protected characteristic, as has been the case in the UK since 2020, when a judge in Norwich Employment Tribunal first ruled that ethical veganism is a philosophical belief under the Equality Act 2010.
sentences.zh: 此案现在是素食主义被接受为受保护特征的其他法律案件的补充，就像英国自 2020 年以来的情况一样，当时诺维奇就业法庭的一名法官首次裁定，道德素食主义是《2010 年平等法案》下的一种哲学信仰。

sentences.en: The ECHR judges mentioned this case in their written judgement, as well as a 2021 case in Bologna, Italy, that was also influenced by the UK case.
sentences.zh: 欧洲人权法院的法官在书面判决中提到了这一案件，以及2021年在意大利博洛尼亚发生的一起案件，该案件也受到了英国案件的影响。

content_html_zh:
2026 年 7 月 16 日，欧洲人权法院 (ECHR) 裁定，瑞士侵犯了两名纯素食者的基本权利，这两名素食者在拘留设施中没有始终如一地获得纯素餐点，确认道德纯素食主义和反物种主义是欧洲境内受保护的哲学信仰。

该法院是欧洲委员会而非欧盟的一部分，因此非欧盟欧洲国家瑞士也受其管辖（英国、挪威和土耳其也是如此）。 瑞士案件始于一名动物权利活动人士于 2018 年 11 月因其直接行动活动被捕，并在日内瓦 Champ-Dollon 监狱审前拘留 11 个月。 他将政府告上法庭，因为他没有获得足够营养的纯素饮食。 2020 年 6 月，瑞士联邦法院认为上诉不可受理，随后囚犯的律师将案件提交给位于斯特拉斯堡的欧洲人权法院。 当时，瑞士一家精神病医院的一名前患者加入了上诉，该上诉由欧洲人权法院处理，因为它已经受理了此案。

2022年10月，法院要求瑞士政府考虑日内瓦监狱是否违反了《欧洲人权公约》第9条，该条规定“每个人都有思想、良心和宗教自由的权利”。 法院现已确认确实如此。

判决认为，当局侵犯了思想、良心和宗教自由的权利，以及获得有效补救的权利。 判决书第126段指出：“在本案的情况下，并考虑到上述比较研究，法院认为，面对申请人一贯和真正的纯素信仰及其在被剥夺自由期间对纯素饮食的要求，当局有第9条规定的积极义务，在明确的国内法律框架内实质性地解决这些要求。 如果做不到这一点，考虑到第 9 条第 2 款提到的目标（见上文第 118 段），很难认为当局已采取必要措施，在利害攸关的利益之间取得公平平衡。 至于审查此类请求时要考虑的因素，包括申请人的利益，但也包括当局可能面临的组织、财务或实际限制。”

此案现在是素食主义被接受为受保护特征的其他法律案件的补充，就像英国自 2020 年以来的情况一样，当时诺维奇就业法庭的一名法官首次裁定，道德素食主义是《2010 年平等法案》下的一种哲学信仰。 欧洲人权法院的法官在书面判决中提到了这一案件，以及2021年在意大利博洛尼亚发生的一起案件，该案件也受到了英国案件的影响。
